situational_context: Running comeback Week 7, consolidation phase. 10 runs completed since gap broken May 3 (2.5 km → 5.8 km progression). ACWR elevated at 1.35 after Run #9 spike (load 34) — consolidating at reduced volume to bring ratio below 1.3. Run #10 today (4.9 km, FC 136, suffer 18) confirmed controlled effort. Chronic load paradoxically lower than May (17.5 vs 22.3) due to inconsistent weekly volume. Weight continues steady decline: 102.6 kg (-1.5 kg since May 14). VO2max running improved to 40.6 (was 38.3 at comeback start). Body battery consistently low (38-48 range, never ≥50 this week) — accumulated fatigue signal. HRV 7d avg 35.5, within 1σ of 90d baseline (36.3 ± 3.2) but trending low. Training status: Functional Overreaching (Garmin). Heat wave incoming Jun 17-20 (33-37°C Paris) — all outdoor sessions need heat adaptation. Primary modality remains e-bike commuting (~38 km round trip on 'sur site' days). Léonie ~5 months old.

recommendations.text: Consolidate ACWR before increasing running volume. Maintain runs at 4-4.5 km until ACWR drops below 1.2. Use ebike (not gravel) for sur site commutes to limit additional load.
recommendations.measurable: ACWR ≤ 1.20 by Jun 21. No run > 4.5 km this week.

recommendations.text: Heat adaptation protocol for all outdoor sessions Jun 17-21: carry ≥500 mL water, reduce FC targets by 5-10 bpm, prefer shaded routes (Bois de Vincennes sous-bois), abort if FC exceeds zone ceiling despite slowing.
recommendations.measurable: No session with average HR >5 bpm above zone target during heat wave.

recommendations.text: Protein intake averaging 129 g/day vs 150 g target. At 102.6 kg during a running comeback with weight loss, protein ≥1.6 g/kg (164 g) is ideal for preserving muscle mass. Prioritise protein at breakfast (currently lowest meal at ~250 kcal).
recommendations.measurable: ≥4 days this week with protein ≥150 g. Track via oh_daily_nutrition_summary.

markers_to_watch.threshold: Alert if ACWR remains > 1.30 for >5 consecutive days
markers_to_watch.reason: Currently 1.35, above safe zone. Return-to-running protocol: 1.30-1.50 → reduce cycling/running volume.

markers_to_watch.threshold: Alert if body battery max stays <50 for >7 consecutive days
markers_to_watch.reason: Has been <50 all week (38-48 range). Indicates accumulated fatigue. Combined with Functional Overreaching status.

markers_to_watch.threshold: Target gradual increase back toward 20-25 over next 2 weeks
markers_to_watch.reason: Dropped from 22.3 (May 14) to 17.5 — inconsistent weekly volume. Need more consistent baseline.

markers_to_watch.threshold: HR drift >10 bpm above normal for same effort in heat (>32°C)
markers_to_watch.reason: Heat wave Jun 17-20. Cardiac drift in heat is expected; excessive drift signals dehydration or heat stress.
